heading: Misleading domain names on the Internet
label: 2252B Misleading domain names on the Internet
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (b). substituted “10 years” for “4 years”.Pub. L. 109–248
amendments: Amendments2006—Subsec. (b). substituted “10 years” for “4 years”.Pub. L. 109–248

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever knowingly uses a misleading domain name on the Internet with the intent to deceive a person into viewing material constituting obscenity shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 2 years, or both.

children.label: b
children.text: Whoever knowingly uses a misleading domain name on the Internet with the intent to deceive a minor into viewing material that is harmful to minors on the Internet shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 10 years, or both.

children.label: c
children.text: For the purposes of this section, a domain name that includes a word or words to indicate the sexual content of the site, such as “sex” or “porn”, is not misleading.
predominantly appeals to a prurient interest of minors;
is patently offensive to prevailing standards in the adult community as a whole with respect to what is suitable material for minors; and
lacks serious literary, artistic, political, or scientific value for minors.

children.label: e
children.text: For the purposes of subsection (d), the term “sex” means acts of masturbation, sexual intercourse, or physcial contact with a person’s genitals, or the condition of human male or female genitals when in a state of sexual stimulation or arousal.1So in original. Probably should be “physical”.1
